question: Describe the cross-sectional view of the configuration consisting of 12 modules.

nuscale_ch01_0068.title: Electrical and Instrumentation and Control Drawings
nuscale_ch01_0068.summary: This section explains that the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report includes simplified electrical, instrumentation, and control (I&C) drawings to visually clarify design information alongside the text. It lists the types of diagrams provided, references tables and figures for diagram legends, and notes that applicants referencing the NuScale design must supply site-specific diagrams and legends as needed.

nuscale_ch01_0372.title: SRP 9.4.1, Rev 3: Control Room Area Ventilation SystemII.4 Control Room
nuscale_ch01_0372.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with SRP 9.4.1, Rev 3 requirements for the Control Room Area Ventilation System. It confirms that the system meets regulatory criteria for protection against natural phenomena, environmental effects, and control room sharing, and clarifies that the control room habitability system uses compressed air tanks for clean air rather than emergency filtration during accidents. The review also notes that certain criteria related to ESF atmosphere cleanup systems are not applicable to the NuScale design.

nuscale_ch01_0039.title: Technical Support Center
nuscale_ch01_0039.summary: The Technical Support Center (TSC) is designed in compliance with NUREG-0696 requirements and provides additional support as detailed in Section 13.3. It functions alongside the Main Control Room (MCR), which uses advanced digital control systems and human-factored displays to enable operators to monitor, control, and respond to plant operations efficiently. The TSC enhances overall plant safety and coordination during both normal and emergency conditions.

nuscale_ch01_0051.title: Plant Features of Special Interest
nuscale_ch01_0051.summary: This section describes key plant features of special interest in the NuScale Power Plant, including the Central Utility Building (CUB) and Diesel Generator Buildings (DGBs), which house essential but nonsafety-related equipment and backup systems. It also highlights human factors engineering (HFE) considerations, emphasizing fail-safe design, automation, digital controls, and workload management to minimize human error and meet regulatory requirements.

nuscale_ch01_0370.title: SRP 9.4.1, Rev 3: Control Room Area Ventilation SystemII.2 Environmental and Dynamic Effects
nuscale_ch01_0370.summary: The section confirms that the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report meets the requirements of SRP 9.4.1, Rev 3, specifically regarding environmental and dynamic effects on the Control Room Area Ventilation System. It states that the system conforms to regulatory criteria, with no exceptions noted for this aspect.

nuscale_ch01_0062.title: Control Rod Assembly Drop and Control Rod Drive Shaft Alignment Test
nuscale_ch01_0062.summary: This section describes the testing and validation of the NuScale control rod assembly (CRA) drop and drive shaft alignment, focusing on the reliability and repeatability of the new remote disconnect mechanism. Testing at Curtiss Wright facilities demonstrated that the remote disconnect system reliably engages and disengages the CRA, and the results have informed improvements in drive rod position detection circuitry, though they do not establish the final design basis. The NPM design features longer, remotely disconnectable drive shafts with multiple alignment supports to ensure proper operation.

nuscale_ch01_0069.title: Piping and Instrumentation Diagrams
nuscale_ch01_0069.summary: This section describes the use of simplified electrical, instrumentation and control (I&C), and mechanical drawings in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report to visually clarify design information. It lists the relevant diagrams and legends used for both I&C and piping and instrumentation diagrams, and notes that site-specific diagrams and legends will be provided by COL applicants referencing the NuScale design certification.

nuscale_ch01_0015.title: General Plant Description
nuscale_ch01_0015.summary: This section provides an overview of the NuScale plant design, summarizing key systems such as safety features, power conversion, fuel handling, cooling, and waste management. It explains the distinction between certified and site-specific design elements, noting that some information is representative or conceptual and marked accordingly, while security-related details are withheld per regulations.

nuscale_ch01_0067.title: Drawings and Other Detailed Information
nuscale_ch01_0067.summary: This section describes the inclusion of simplified instrumentation, control, electrical, and mechanical drawings in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report to visually clarify design information alongside written descriptions. It references tables and figures listing and explaining the symbols used in these diagrams, and notes that applicants referencing the NuScale design must provide additional site-specific diagrams and legends as needed.

nuscale_ch01_0120.title: 1.8.2 Departures
nuscale_ch01_0120.summary: Section 1.8.2 describes the requirement for a Combined License (COL) applicant referencing the NuScale Power Plant design certification to provide a list of any departures from the certified design. This ensures that any differences between the certified design and the site-specific implementation are clearly identified and addressed in the licensing process.

nuscale_ch01_0073.title: Departures
nuscale_ch01_0073.summary: This section outlines the interface requirements between the NuScale Power Plant’s certified design and the site-specific design provided in the combined license (COL) application. It explains the types of interface requirements (CDI and COL), the identification of COL information items, and notes that COL applicants must provide a list of any departures from the certified design. Tables and figures referenced in the section help clarify the boundaries and responsibilities between certified and site-specific elements.

nuscale_ch01_0117.title: Figure 1.7-3f: Piping and In strumentation Diagram Legends
nuscale_ch01_0117.summary: This section provides the legend for symbols used in NuScale's Piping and Instrumentation Diagrams (P&IDs), detailing the graphical representations for various instruments, sensors, and auxiliary devices. It includes symbols for flow, pressure, temperature, and level measurement devices, as well as sample analysis and burner monitoring equipment, ensuring standardized interpretation of P&ID drawings.

nuscale_ch01_0072.title: Combined License Information Items
nuscale_ch01_0072.summary: This section explains the interface requirements between the NuScale Power Plant certified design and the site-specific design provided in the combined license (COL) application. It distinguishes between conceptual design information (CDI) for non-certified plant portions and COL information items, which are site-specific responsibilities of the COL applicant. The section also notes that COL applicants must address all COL information items and provide a list of any departures from the certified design.

edges.description: Section A provides detailed drawings and figures that visually clarify the design configuration, which can be used to illustrate or support the regulatory conformance statements about the control room area ventilation system and module configuration described in Section B.

Section A provides drawings and figures that visually clarify design information, which can include the cross-sectional configuration of the 12 modules described in Section B's general plant description.

Section A provides detailed drawings and figures that visually clarify the design configuration, which can include the cross-sectional view of the 12-module configuration described in Section B's general plant features.

Section A provides detailed drawings and figures that can visually clarify the configuration of the 12-module system, which supports Section B's regulatory conformance statements regarding environmental and dynamic effects for the control room area ventilation system.

Section A provides drawings and figures that visually clarify the design configuration, which can be used to understand the physical arrangement relevant to the module configuration described in the analysis question, while Section B discusses testing and alignment of specific components (control rod assemblies) that would be depicted in such drawings.

Section A outlines regulatory conformance for the control room area ventilation system, which is relevant to Section B's description of the technical support center and its monitoring of multiple reactor modules, as both sections address the configuration and operational oversight of shared systems in a multi-module plant.

Section A outlines regulatory criteria and conformance for control room area ventilation systems, while Section B describes the physical plant features, including the Central Utility Building that houses utility services potentially relevant to the configuration of the 12 modules.

Section A references Section B by listing SRP 9.4.1, Rev 3: Control Room Area Ventilation SystemII.2 Environmental and Dynamic Effects as a regulatory criterion with which the design must conform, thereby connecting the general conformance summary (A) to the specific environmental and dynamic effects requirements (B).

Section A describes the digital control system displays for each reactor module, while Section B references regulatory criteria (SRP 9.4.1) related to the control room area ventilation system, both of which are relevant to the configuration and operation of a multi-module plant such as the 12-module configuration in the analysis question.

Section A describes the digital control systems and operator interfaces for monitoring each reactor module, while Section B details testing and validation of the control rod drive mechanisms that are part of the reactor module configuration, connecting them through the operational and safety assurance of the 12-module plant design.

Section A describes the Central Utility Building and its housed systems, which are relevant to the configuration of the 12 modules, while Section B references regulatory criteria (SRP 9.4.1) that the plant's systems, including those in the CUB, must conform to, thus connecting the physical configuration to regulatory requirements.

Section A describes the Central Utility Building (CUB), which is a plant structure that may house systems relevant to the configuration of the 12 modules referenced in the analysis question, while Section B discusses testing of control rod assemblies that are components within those modules, thus both sections provide information about different aspects (infrastructure and component testing) of the 12-module configuration.

Section A outlines regulatory criteria and conformance for ventilation and containment systems, while Section B details testing and validation of control rod assembly alignment, with both sections connected through their relevance to the overall safety and configuration of the 12-module system described in the analysis question.

Section A provides a general overview of the types of drawings included (such as piping, instrumentation, electrical, and mechanical), while Section B specifies the subset focused on electrical and instrumentation and control drawings, both referencing the same source and context.

Section A provides detailed drawings and diagrams that visually clarify the design configuration, which can be used to describe the cross-sectional view of the 12-module configuration referenced in the analysis question, while Section B lists regulatory criteria and conformance statements that the design (including the configuration) must meet.

Section A provides detailed drawings and diagrams that visually clarify the design configuration, which supports the general plant description in Section B by offering the specific cross-sectional information needed to describe the 12-module configuration.

Section A provides a general overview of the plant's configuration, while Section B offers detailed drawings that visually clarify and specify the design aspects summarized in Section A, including the arrangement of the 12 modules.

Section A provides a general overview of the plant's configuration, including the arrangement of modules, which forms the basis for understanding specific systems like the control room ventilation described in Section B.

Section A provides a general overview of the plant's configuration, including the existence of multiple modules, while Section B details the digital control systems used to monitor and manage each reactor module, thus specifying how the general configuration is operated and supervised.

Section A provides detailed drawings and figures that visually clarify the design described in the general overview given in Section B, specifically supporting the understanding of the 12-module configuration mentioned in the analysis question.

Section A provides a general overview of the types of drawings and detailed information available, while Section B specifically refers to the electrical and instrumentation and control drawings that are part of the detailed information described in Section A.

Section A provides detailed drawings and figures that visually clarify the design configuration of the 12 modules, which supports Section B's regulatory conformance statements by serving as evidence that the design meets the requirements for control room area ventilation and related systems.

Section A provides detailed drawings and figures that visually clarify the design configuration, which supports the description in Section B regarding the arrangement and monitoring of the 12 reactor modules in the plant.

Section A references general interface requirements and points to figures that represent the overall facility configuration, while Section B describes the provision of detailed drawings (including figures) that visually clarify design information such as the cross-sectional view of the 12-module configuration.

Section A identifies and describes the certified design boundaries and interfaces, which provide the context for understanding how the configuration of 12 modules (including their cross-sectional arrangement) relates to the regulatory conformance statements in Section B regarding shared structures and systems such as the Control Room Area Ventilation System.

Section A references the general facility representation and boundaries (including figures) that are further detailed in Section B's description of digital control system displays for each reactor module, connecting the overall configuration to specific monitoring provisions for the 12-module setup.
Section A identifies the overall facility configuration and boundaries, while Section B provides detailed drawings that visually clarify the design information referenced in Section A.

Section A identifies and describes the certified design boundaries and interfaces, which provide the context and scope for the systems (such as the Control Room Area Ventilation System) discussed in Section B, thereby specifying which parts of the facility's configuration are relevant to the regulatory criteria addressed in Section B.

Section A references Figure 1.2-1 and Figure 1.2-2, which provide visual representations of the overall facility and boundaries relevant to the configuration of 12 modules described in the analysis question, while Section B describes the digital control systems and monitoring displays associated with each reactor module, thus both sections provide complementary information about the configuration and operation of the 12-module setup.

Section A provides detailed drawings and figures that visually clarify the design configuration, which can be used to describe the cross-sectional view of the 12-module configuration referenced in the analysis question, while Section B outlines the interface requirements and identifies which structures, systems, and components are part of the certified design, thus specifying the scope and boundaries relevant to interpreting the drawings in Section A.

Section A provides detailed drawings and figures that visually clarify the design configuration, which can be used to describe the cross-sectional view of the 12-module configuration referenced in the analysis question, while Section B defines the interface boundaries and identifies which structures, systems, and components (including the 12 modules) are part of the certified design, thus establishing the scope for which Section A's drawings are applicable.
Section A provides the specific diagram legends necessary to interpret the types of symbols and components shown in the general electrical and instrumentation drawings described in Section B.

Section A provides diagram legends for interpreting piping and instrumentation drawings, which are necessary for understanding the detailed configuration referenced in Section B's regulatory conformance statements about the control room area ventilation system.

Section A provides diagram legends for interpreting piping and instrumentation drawings, which are necessary for understanding the cross-sectional configuration of the 12-module system described in Section B's general plant description.

Section A identifies the overall facility configuration and references figures that represent the facility layout, while Section B provides the detailed drawings (including cross-sectional views) that visually clarify the configuration described in Section A.

Section A identifies and describes the boundaries and interfaces of the certified design, which includes the configuration of the 12 modules, while Section B details regulatory criteria and conformance for systems (such as the Control Room Area Ventilation System) that are part of or interact with those modules, thus Section A provides the structural context for the systems addressed in Section B.

Section A outlines the interface requirements and identifies the boundaries and components of the certified design, which includes the configuration of 12 modules referenced in the analysis question, while Section B describes the control and monitoring systems that are implemented for each reactor module within that configuration.

Section A provides diagram legends used to interpret detailed drawings, which are necessary for understanding the cross-sectional configuration referenced in Section B's discussion of certified design interfaces.
